title: Remote Listing — Socket Server
description: Add network awareness to your file manager. Create a RemoteFileServer class that serves directory listings over a socket, and a request_listing() client function that connects and retrieves the listing. This introduces socket-based client-server programming.
test_code:
assert callable(RemoteFileServer), 'RemoteFileServer should be a callable class'
assert callable(request_listing), 'request_listing should be a callable function'
import inspect
init_sig = inspect.signature(RemoteFileServer.__init__)
params = list(init_sig.parameters.keys())
assert 'directory' in params, 'RemoteFileServer.__init__ should accept a directory parameter'
assert 'socket' in sys.modules or 'socket' in dir(), 'socket module should be imported'
server = RemoteFileServer('.', 'localhost', 0)
assert hasattr(server, 'directory'), 'Server should have a directory attribute'
assert hasattr(server, 'host'), 'Server should have a host attribute'
assert callable(getattr(server, 'start', None)), 'Server must have start method'
assert callable(getattr(server, 'stop', None)), 'Server must have stop method'
assert callable(getattr(server, 'get_port', None)), 'Server must have get_port method'
acceptance_criteria: RemoteFileServer class accepts directory, host, port parameters
Server has start(), stop(), and get_port() methods
request_listing() is a standalone function that connects to the server
hints: Bind and listen: self.server_socket.bind((host, port)), self.server_socket.listen(1)
